abstract: Creates an object for resolving a list of remote processes.

declaration.code: func AECreateRemoteProcessResolver(_ allocator: CFAllocator!, _ url: CFURL!) -> AERemoteProcessResolverRef!

overview:
## Return Value

An [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/aeremoteprocessresolverref], which must be disposed of with [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1442572-aedisposeremoteprocessresolver]. A resolver can only be used one time; once it has obtained a list of remote processes from a server, or gotten an error, it can no longer be scheduled. To retrieve a new list of processes, create a new instance of this object.

## Discussion

You supply this function with the URL for a remote host and port; it returns a reference to a resolver object. To obtain a list of remote processes from the resolver, you can query it synchronously with [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1444456-aeremoteprocessresolvergetproces], which blocks until the request completes (either successfully or with an error).

If asynchronous behavior is desired, you can optionally use [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1447259-aeremoteprocessresolverschedulew] to schedule the resolver asynchronously on a run loop. If so, you supply a callback routine (see [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/aeremoteprocessresolvercallback]) that is executed when the resolver completes. To obtain information about the remote processes, you will again have to call [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1444456-aeremoteprocessresolvergetproces]. 

A resolver can only be used once; once it has fetched the data or gotten an error it can no longer be scheduled. The data obtained by the resolver is a `CFArrayRef` of `CFDictionaryRef` objects. For information on the format of the returned remote process information, see the description of the function result for the function [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1444456-aeremoteprocessresolvergetproces], and also [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/applicationservices/apple_event_manager/remote_process_dictionary_keys].

### Version-Notes

Thread safe starting in OS X v10.3.

# AECreateRemoteProcessResolver(_:_:)

**Function**

Creates an object for resolving a list of remote processes.

## Declaration

```swift
func AECreateRemoteProcessResolver(_ allocator: CFAllocator!, _ url: CFURL!) -> AERemoteProcessResolverRef!
```

## Parameters

- **allocator**: An object that is used to allocates and deallocate any Core Foundation types created or returned by this API. You can pass `kCFAllocatorDefault` to get the default allocation behavior. The allocator is based on `CFAllocatorRef`, an opaque data type described in the Core Foundation Reference Documentation.
- **url**: A `CFURL` reference identifying the remote host and port on which to look for processes. See the Core Foundation Reference Documentation for a description of the `CFURLRef` data type.

## Return Value

An [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/aeremoteprocessresolverref], which must be disposed of with [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1442572-aedisposeremoteprocessresolver]. A resolver can only be used one time; once it has obtained a list of remote processes from a server, or gotten an error, it can no longer be scheduled. To retrieve a new list of processes, create a new instance of this object.

## Discussion

You supply this function with the URL for a remote host and port; it returns a reference to a resolver object. To obtain a list of remote processes from the resolver, you can query it synchronously with [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1444456-aeremoteprocessresolvergetproces], which blocks until the request completes (either successfully or with an error).

If asynchronous behavior is desired, you can optionally use [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1447259-aeremoteprocessresolverschedulew] to schedule the resolver asynchronously on a run loop. If so, you supply a callback routine (see [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/aeremoteprocessresolvercallback]) that is executed when the resolver completes. To obtain information about the remote processes, you will again have to call [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1444456-aeremoteprocessresolvergetproces]. 

A resolver can only be used once; once it has fetched the data or gotten an error it can no longer be scheduled. The data obtained by the resolver is a `CFArrayRef` of `CFDictionaryRef` objects. For information on the format of the returned remote process information, see the description of the function result for the function [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1444456-aeremoteprocessresolvergetproces], and also [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/applicationservices/apple_event_manager/remote_process_dictionary_keys].

### Version-Notes

Thread safe starting in OS X v10.3.

## Locating Processes on Remote Computers

- **AEDisposeRemoteProcessResolver(_:)**: Disposes of an `AERemoteProcessResolverRef`.
- **AERemoteProcessResolverGetProcesses(_:_:)**: Returns an array of objects containing information about processes running on a remote machine.
- **AERemoteProcessResolverScheduleWithRunLoop(_:_:_:_:_:)**: Schedules a resolver for execution on a given run loop in a given mode.
